name: Mexican Wedding Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Beat butter until light and fluffy.
2. Gradually beat in 2 tablespoons of powdered sugar, egg yolk, vanilla, and almonds.
3. Mix in the remaining powdered sugar gradually with the all-purpose flour, beating to blend thoroughly.
4. Pinch off pieces of dough the size of large walnuts and roll into round balls between your palms.
5. Place the balls about 1 1/2 inches apart on ungreased baking sheets; flatten each ball slightly.
6. Bake in a 275°F oven until very lightly browned, about 45 minutes.
7. Let cool on baking sheets until lukewarm.
8. Sift half the remaining powdered sugar onto a large sheet of wax paper.
9. Roll each cookie gently in the sugar.
10. Pack more sugar over the cookies to a depth of about 1/8 inch with your fingers.
11. Place the cookies on wire racks and dust generously with more powdered sugar.
12. Let cool completely before storing in airtight containers, layered between sheets of wax paper.
Makes 3 dozen.
